title: SpaceX Wants To Power Pentagon AI. Microsoft, Amazon And Google Already Do.
excerpt: Rocket manufacturer Space Exploration Technologies Corp. is expanding beyond aerospace and into a business dominated by Big Tech, as the company explores lea...
content_html:
Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

Rocket manufacturer Space Exploration Technologies Corp. is expanding beyond aerospace and into a business dominated by Big Tech, as the company explores leasing AI computing power to the U.S. government.

According to The Wall Street Journal, Elon Musk's SpaceX and the Pentagon are discussing a leasing agreement under which the company would rent computing capacity from its data centers, allowing military and intelligence agencies to run AI models on SpaceX's infrastructure.

Neither SpaceX nor the Defense Department has commented on the potential deal. But if it moves forward, SpaceX would essentially enter a market already served by Microsoft Corporation, Amazon.com, Inc., Alphabet Inc's Google and Oracle Corp — all of which provide cloud infrastructure to the Pentagon under the Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability program.

Don't Miss:

  • A single bad hire can set a startup back years. Here are the 5 hires founders most often misjudge — and why

  • Still Learning the Market? These 50 Must-Know Terms Can Help You Catch Up Fast

From Launching Rockets to Powering Pentagon AI

The Pentagon is already one of SpaceX's largest customers. The company launches national security missions, provides military satellite communications through Starlink and supports missile-tracking initiatives. Leasing AI computing power would deepen that relationship, expanding SpaceX's role from delivering physical infrastructure to providing the computing infrastructure that powers artificial intelligence.

For investors, that marks another step in the company's evolution.

SpaceX has steadily diversified beyond launch services through Starlink, turning connectivity into a major business. AI computing could become another high-growth revenue stream as demand for compute continues to outpace supply.

A New Competitive Landscape

Unlike the launch industry, where SpaceX has established a commanding lead, AI infrastructure is one of the most competitive markets in technology.

See Also: Avoid the #1 Investing Mistake: How Your 'Safe' Holdings Could Be Costing You Big Time

Microsoft's Azure, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud and Oracle have spent years building cloud platforms for enterprise and government customers. A Pentagon AI compute deal would place SpaceX alongside those hyperscalers rather than traditional aerospace and defense contractors.

That shift reflects a broader trend across the AI industry. As computing power becomes one of the world's most valuable resources, the lines between aerospace, cloud computing and artificial intelligence are beginning to blur.

content_html_zh:
Benzinga 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可能通过以下链接赚取某些项目的佣金或收入。

火箭制造商太空探索技术公司 (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) 正在探索将人工智能计算能力租赁给美国政府，该公司正在将业务范围从航空航天领域扩展到以大型科技公司为主的业务。

据《华尔街日报》报道，埃隆·马斯克旗下的 SpaceX 和五角大楼正在讨论一项租赁协议，根据该协议，该公司将从其数据中心租用计算能力，允许军事和情报机构在 SpaceX 的基础设施上运行人工智能模型。

SpaceX 和国防部均未对这笔潜在交易发表评论。 但如果向前推进，SpaceX 基本上将进入微软公司、亚马逊公司、Alphabet 公司旗下的谷歌和甲骨文公司已经服务的市场——所有这些公司都根据联合作战云能力计划向五角大楼提供云基础设施。

不要错过：

  • 一次糟糕的招聘可能会让一家初创企业倒退数年。 以下是创始人最常误判的 5 名员工——以及原因
  • 还在学习市场吗？ 这 50 个必须知道的术语可以帮助您快速跟上

从发射火箭到为五角大楼人工智能提供动力

五角大楼已经是 SpaceX 最大的客户之一。 该公司执行国家安全任务，通过星链提供军事卫星通信并支持导弹跟踪计划。 租赁人工智能计算能力将加深这种关系，将 SpaceX 的角色从提供物理基础设施扩展到提供支持人工智能的计算基础设施。

对于投资者来说，这标志着公司发展的又一步。

SpaceX 通过 Starlink 稳步实现发射服务以外的多元化发展，将连接转变为一项主要业务。 随着计算需求持续超过供应，人工智能计算可能成为另一个高增长收入来源。

新的竞争格局

与 SpaceX 在发射行业占据绝对领先地位不同，人工智能基础设施是技术领域竞争最激烈的市场之一。

另请参阅：避免第一大投资错误：您的“安全”资产可能会花费您大量时间

微软的Azure、亚马逊网络服务、谷歌云和甲骨文花了数年时间为企业和政府客户构建云平台。 五角大楼的人工智能计算交易将使 SpaceX 与这些超大规模企业并肩作战，而不是传统的航空航天和国防承包商。

这种转变反映了整个人工智能行业的更广泛趋势。 随着计算能力成为世界上最有价值的资源之一，航空航天、云计算和人工智能之间的界限开始变得模糊。
